rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving visible ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reside in valid AADL scopes: component types for general assumptions/guarantees, implementations for subcomponent-level bindings.
Avoid introducing package-qualified or undefined identifiers in AGREE annexes; rely solely on model-extracted symbols.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references or subcomponent paths in a component type annex; move them to the implementation scope or remove them.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly defined as data ports in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair actions
Model analyst tasks such as AADL structural extraction
